questions.promptText: Find the next number in the series: 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, ?
Find the next number in the series: 3, 9, 27, 81, ?
questions.explanation: Each term is multiplied by 3: 81x3=243.

questions.promptText: If CAT is coded as DBU, how is DOG coded?
questions.explanation: Each letter is shifted forward by 1: D->E, O->P, G->H.

questions.promptText: All roses are flowers. Some flowers fade quickly. Conclusion: Some roses fade quickly.
questions.explanation: The premises don't establish that any rose is among the flowers that fade quickly.

questions.promptText: Find the next term: A, C, F, J, O, ?
questions.explanation: Gaps increase by 1 each time: +2,+3,+4,+5,+6, so O+6=U.

questions.promptText: Pointing to a photograph, a man says, 'She is the daughter of my grandfather's only son.' How is the girl related to the man?
questions.explanation: 'My grandfather's only son' is the man's father, so his daughter is the man's sister.

questions.promptText: Ravi walks 5 km north, then turns right and walks 3 km, then turns right again and walks 5 km. How far is he from the starting point?
questions.explanation: He ends up 3 km east of the start, since the two 5 km legs cancel out in direction.

questions.promptText: Find the next number in the series: 1, 4, 9, 16, 25, ?
questions.explanation: These are perfect squares: 6^2=36.

questions.promptText: In a code language, 'ROSE' is written as 'URVH'. How is 'LOTUS' written?
questions.explanation: Each letter is shifted forward by 3: L->O, O->R, T->W, U->X, S->V.

questions.promptText: No cats are dogs. All dogs are animals. Conclusion: No cats are animals.
questions.explanation: This is an invalid syllogism; cats could still be animals even though no cats are dogs.

questions.promptText: Find the odd one out: 11, 13, 17, 19, 22, 23
questions.explanation: All the others are prime numbers; 22 is not prime.

questions.promptText: A is B's brother. C is B's mother. D is C's father. How is A related to D?
questions.explanation: D is C's father, so D is the grandfather of C's children, including A.

questions.promptText: Find the next term: AZ, BY, CX, DW, ?
questions.explanation: First letters go forward (A,B,C,D,E), second letters go backward (Z,Y,X,W,V).

questions.promptText: Doctor : Hospital :: Teacher : ?
questions.explanation: A doctor works at a hospital, just as a teacher works at a school.

questions.promptText: Find the next number: 2, 5, 10, 17, 26, ?
questions.explanation: Pattern is n^2+1: 6^2+1=37.

questions.promptText: If PAPER is coded as QBQFS, how is PENCIL coded?
questions.explanation: Each letter is shifted forward by 1: P->Q, E->F, N->O, C->D, I->J, L->M.

questions.promptText: A man walks 4 km east, then 3 km north. How far is he from the starting point?
questions.explanation: By the Pythagorean theorem, distance = sqrt(4^2+3^2) = 5 km.

questions.promptText: Find the next number: 6, 11, 21, 36, 56, ?
questions.explanation: The differences increase by 5 each time (5,10,15,20,25), so 56+25=81.

questions.promptText: In a class of 40 students, Rohan ranks 14th from the top. What is his rank from the bottom?
questions.explanation: Rank from bottom = total - rank from top + 1 = 40-14+1=27.

questions.promptText: A is older than B but younger than C. D is older than C. Who is the oldest?
questions.explanation: The order is D > C > A > B, so D is the oldest.

questions.promptText: In a row of children, Meena is 7th from the left and 12th from the right. How many children are there in total?
questions.explanation: Total = position from left + position from right - 1 = 7+12-1=18.

questions.promptText: Five boxes are stacked: P above Q, Q above R, R above S, S above T. Which box is exactly in the middle?
questions.explanation: Order top to bottom is P,Q,R,S,T, so R is the middle (3rd of 5).

questions.promptText: If Monday falls on the 3rd of a month, what day will fall on the 24th of the same month?
questions.explanation: 24-3=21 days later, and 21 is exactly divisible by 7, so it's also a Monday.

questions.promptText: A is twice as old as B. Five years ago, A was three times as old as B. Find B's current age.
questions.explanation: Let B=x, A=2x. Then 2x-5=3(x-5), giving x=10.

questions.promptText: In a family, P is the father of Q. Q is the sister of R. R is the son of S. How is S related to P?
questions.explanation: S is the mother of Q and R, and P is their father, so S is P's wife.

questions.promptText: Four friends P, Q, R, S have different heights. P is taller than Q but shorter than R. S is the tallest. Who is the shortest?
questions.explanation: Order is S > R > P > Q, so Q is the shortest.

questions.promptText: A number when divided by 5 leaves remainder 3. What will be the remainder when the square of the number is divided by 5?
questions.explanation: n=5k+3, so n^2=25k^2+30k+9, which mod 5 leaves remainder 4.

questions.promptText: If TABLE is coded using alphabet positions as 20-1-2-12-5, what is the code for CHAIR?
questions.explanation: Each letter's position in the alphabet: C=3,H=8,A=1,I=9,R=18.

questions.promptText: If today is Wednesday, what will be the day after 61 days?
questions.explanation: 61 mod 7 = 5, and Wednesday + 5 days = Monday.

questions.promptText: A is the mother of B. B is the husband of C. C is the mother of D. How is A related to D?
questions.explanation: A is B's mother, B and C are D's parents, so A is D's grandmother.

questions.promptText: In a queue, Raj is 15th from the front and 8th from the back. How many people are in the queue?
The ratio of ages of A and B is 3:4. After 5 years, the ratio becomes 4:5. Find A's current age.
questions.explanation: Solving (3x+5):(4x+5)=4:5 gives x=5, so A's age = 3x=15.

questions.promptText: Five people stand in a line by height, tallest first. Sneha is shorter than Aman but taller than Raj. Priya is the tallest. Kunal is shorter than Raj. Who is the shortest?
questions.explanation: Order is Priya > Aman > Sneha > Raj > Kunal, so Kunal is shortest.

questions.promptText: Count the number of times '8' is immediately followed by '9' in this sequence: 4 8 9 3 8 9 1 8 2 9 8 9 5
questions.explanation: The pairs '8,9' occur at three positions in the sequence.

questions.promptText: Which of the following words is spelled correctly?
questions.explanation: The correct spelling follows 'i before e except after c': Receive.

questions.promptText: Find the number that does not belong: 4, 9, 16, 26, 36
questions.explanation: The others are perfect squares (4,9,16,36); 26 breaks the pattern (should be 25).

questions.promptText: Identify the item that is different from the rest: Apple, Banana, Carrot, Mango, Orange
questions.explanation: Carrot is a vegetable while the others are fruits.

questions.promptText: How many times does the letter 'S' appear in the word 'MISSISSIPPI'?
questions.explanation: The word contains the letter S four times.

questions.promptText: Identify the pair that does not match the pattern: 3-9, 4-16, 5-20, 6-36
questions.explanation: The pattern is n-n^2; 5 should pair with 25, not 20.

questions.promptText: Two numbers 74829164 and 74829174 differ only slightly. At which digit position (from the left) do they first differ?
questions.explanation: Comparing digit by digit, the two numbers first differ at the 7th position (6 vs 7).

questions.promptText: Which of these dates is invalid?
questions.explanation: February never has 30 days, even in a leap year.

questions.promptText: Find the odd one out based on spelling: Necessary, Occassion, Definitely, Separate
questions.explanation: 'Occassion' is misspelled; the correct spelling is 'Occasion'.

questions.promptText: Count the vowels in the word 'ENVIRONMENT'.
questions.explanation: The vowels in ENVIRONMENT are E, I, O, E — a total of 4.

questions.promptText: Which pair of numbers has the same digit sum? 456, 273, 528, 619
Identify the correctly formatted email address.
questions.explanation: A valid email requires a single '@' and a properly formatted domain with a dot.

questions.promptText: Which of the following letters looks the same when viewed in a vertical mirror?
questions.explanation: Letter 'A' is symmetric about a vertical axis, unlike F, P, or G.

questions.promptText: How many triangles are formed when a square is divided by both its diagonals?
questions.explanation: The two diagonals divide the square into 4 triangles.

questions.promptText: A rectangular sheet is folded in half twice (each fold along a different axis), then a hole is punched through the center of all layers. How many holes appear when fully unfolded?
questions.explanation: Two perpendicular folds create 4 layers, so the punch produces 4 holes.

questions.promptText: If the letters of the word 'GARDEN' are arranged in alphabetical order, which letter will be third?
questions.explanation: Sorted letters are A, D, E, G, N, R — the third is E.

questions.promptText: Which of these figures has the most lines of symmetry: a square, a rectangle, an equilateral triangle, or a regular hexagon?
questions.explanation: A regular hexagon has 6 lines of symmetry, more than the others.

questions.promptText: A die is rolled so that 1, 2, 3 are visible on three faces meeting at a corner. If opposite faces of a standard die sum to 7, what is the sum of the hidden faces?
questions.explanation: Total of all faces (1 to 6) is 21; subtracting the visible sum (1+2+3=6) gives 15.

questions.promptText: How many squares of any size are there in a 2x2 grid of unit squares?
questions.explanation: There are 4 unit squares plus 1 larger 2x2 square, totaling 5.

questions.promptText: A clock shows 3:15. What is the angle between the hour and minute hands?
questions.explanation: The minute hand is at 90 degrees and the hour hand at 97.5 degrees, a difference of 7.5 degrees.

questions.promptText: A rectangular block of size 4x3x2 units is painted on all outer faces, then cut into 1x1x1 unit cubes. How many small cubes have exactly one face painted?
questions.explanation: Using the standard formula for a cuboid, exactly 4 unit cubes end up with exactly one painted face.

questions.promptText: Statement: 'The government should ban all plastic bags to reduce pollution.' Which of the following, if true, would most weaken this argument?

options.text: Plastic bags are convenient for shopping
Most pollution comes from industrial waste, not plastic bags
Plastic bags are cheap to produce
Some countries have already banned plastic bags

questions.explanation: If most pollution has another source, banning plastic bags wouldn't meaningfully address the stated goal.

questions.promptText: Argument: 'Since the new policy was introduced, crime rates have fallen. Therefore, the policy caused the decrease in crime.' What is the primary flaw in this reasoning?

options.text: It assumes correlation implies causation without ruling out other factors

questions.explanation: The argument confuses a coincidental correlation in timing with proven causation.

questions.promptText: Statement: 'All successful entrepreneurs take risks. John takes risks. Therefore, John is a successful entrepreneur.' What is the logical flaw?
questions.explanation: Taking risks doesn't guarantee success; the argument wrongly reverses the original conditional.

questions.promptText: Which assumption is necessary for this argument: 'Since the library added more computers, student research output has improved, so the school should add more computers elsewhere too'?

options.text: The improvement was primarily due to the added computers, not other factors
The library is the most used facility

questions.explanation: The argument only holds if the computers, not some other factor, actually caused the improvement.

questions.promptText: Statement: 'Company X's sales increased after it lowered prices, so lowering prices always increases sales.' What weakens this generalization most?

options.text: A competitor's sales also increased during the same period, unrelated to pricing
Company X is a small business
Sales data was collected over one year

questions.explanation: If sales rose for an unrelated reason across the market, the price cut may not be the actual cause.

questions.promptText: Argument: 'If it rains, the match will be postponed. It did not rain. Therefore, the match was not postponed.' What is the flaw?

options.text: Denying the antecedent — the match could be postponed for other reasons

questions.explanation: Not raining doesn't rule out other reasons the match could still be postponed.
